skill.name: gemini-image
skill.description: Generate and edit images from natural-language prompts using a third-party Gemini-style image API. Supports text-to-image, image-to-image, reference URLs, model variants, and aspect-ratio guidance.
skill.summary: Creates images from text prompts and image URLs through an external Gemini-style image service.
skill.author: Ceeon

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported a critical combination, but I did not find confirmed malicious intent or prompt-injection text in the reviewed files. Most backtick and entropy findings are false positives from Markdown formatting and Chinese text. The skill still carries medium risk because it intentionally reads an API key, calls a third-party image API, and recommends uploading local images to public file hosts.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Image API Receives Prompts And API Key
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to read an API key from config/secrets.md and call https://api.apicore.ai/v1/images/generations with an Authorization bearer token. This appears to be intended functionality, but it exposes prompts, image references, and credentials to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API endpoint, Authorization header, and API_KEY placeholder are explicit in the skill instructions. The behavior is legitimate for image generation, but the privacy and credential exposure risk is real.

medium_findings.title: Local Images May Be Uploaded To Public File Hosts
medium_findings.description: The image-to-image workflow tells users to upload local images to Litterbox or Catbox before sending the returned URL in a prompt. This can disclose private or regulated images to third-party public hosting services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-upload commands and public host URLs are directly documented. The risk depends on user image sensitivity, but the disclosure path is clear.

medium_findings.title: API Secret Stored In Local Skill Files
medium_findings.description: The README and example secret file direct users to copy a template and place a real API key in secrets.md under the Claude skills directory. This is a common configuration pattern, but it can expose secrets through weak file permissions, backups, shell history, or accidental commits.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions clearly require a local API-key file. I did not find evidence of exfiltration, so the risk is configuration exposure rather than malicious collection.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Flagged As Command Execution
low_findings.description: Many external-command findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline examples, tables, and prompt templates. The reviewed files do contain shell examples, but I did not find dynamic code execution logic or user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is visible Markdown documentation, not executable Ruby or application source. The only real commands are static installation and curl examples.

low_findings.title: Entropy And Weak-Crypto Heuristics Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-entropy and weak-cryptographic-algorithm detections appear to come from Chinese text and the word gemini in metadata, not from encoded payloads or cryptographic operations. No evidence found for hidden binary content or weak crypto use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found readable Markdown and no encoded blocks, encryption routines, or cryptographic API use. The scanner signal is plausible for multilingual documentation.

low_findings.title: Affiliate Registration URL Requires Disclosure
low_findings.description: The documentation directs users to an external registration URL with an affiliate parameter. This is not a code security issue, but marketplace review should ensure the referral relationship and third-party dependency are disclosed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external registration link and affiliate query parameter are explicit. I did not find evidence that the link is malicious, so this is a transparency and trust concern.

dangerous_patterns.title: curl Request With Authorization Header To External API
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill provides a curl POST request to a third-party image-generation endpoint using a bearer API key. Users should understand that prompts and credentials leave their local environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact endpoint and Authorization header are shown together. This is the core network pattern that creates the credential and prompt-disclosure risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Multipart Upload Of Local Files To Public Hosts
dangerous_patterns.description: The upload guide uses curl multipart file uploads to Catbox and Litterbox. This can publish local image contents outside the user workspace before image generation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands include fileToUpload with a local image path and public upload endpoints. The risk is straightforward even though it supports the stated image-to-image workflow.

content.value_statement: Image generation workflows often require exact API calls, image URLs, and model settings. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through text-to-image and image-to-image requests using a Gemini-style image API.
actual_capabilities: Detects natural-language requests to draw, generate, or create images.
Formats text-to-image prompts for a third-party image generation endpoint.
Supports image-to-image prompts by placing an image URL before the instruction.
Supports multiple reference image URLs in one prompt.
Documents standard, 2K, and 4K model naming options.
Lists common output aspect ratios such as 1:1, 16:9, and 9:16.
limitations: Requires a third-party API key configured in a local secrets file.
Sends prompts, image URLs, and request metadata to an external API.
Local image inputs must first be uploaded to a reachable image URL.
Chinese text inside generated images may need repeated attempts and manual checking.

use_cases.title: Create Concept Images Quickly
use_cases.description: Turn a short visual idea into a generated image with size and model guidance.

use_cases.title: Generate Social And Marketing Visuals
use_cases.description: Produce square, vertical, or widescreen images from campaign prompts.

use_cases.title: Iterate From Reference Images
use_cases.description: Use image URLs as references for enhancement, style transfer, or controlled edits.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Text To Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one generated image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gemini-image to create a 1:1 image of a calm mountain lake at sunrise, soft light, realistic style.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Aspect Ratio
prompt_templates.scenario: Create an image for a specific placement.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gemini-image to generate a 16:9 hero image of a modern workspace with warm natural light and clean composition.

prompt_templates.title: Use A Reference Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Image-to-image workflow after the user provides a URL.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gemini-image with this image URL as the reference: [image URL]. Create a similar style with brighter colors and sharper details.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Multi-Reference Prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: Combine several references with specific output constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gemini-image with these reference URLs: [first URL] [second URL]. Blend the product shape from the first image with the lighting style from the second image, in 4K, 3:2 ratio.

output_examples.input: Generate a 16:9 sunset landscape in a watercolor style.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a text-to-image request, selects the requested aspect ratio, and returns the generated image URL.

output_examples.input: Use this product photo URL and make a cleaner catalog-style image.
output_examples.output: The assistant places the image URL before the edit instruction and returns the new generated image link.

output_examples.input: Fix one Chinese character in a poster image while keeping the rest unchanged.
output_examples.output: The assistant uses the provided Chinese text guidance and asks for a focused edit with minimal changes elsewhere.

best_practices: Use non-sensitive images when uploading references to public image hosts.
Keep API keys out of chat messages, screenshots, logs, and version control.
Specify aspect ratio, model quality, subject, style, and constraints in one clear prompt.
anti_patterns: Do not upload private, regulated, or client-owned images to public hosts without approval.
Do not paste real API keys into prompts or shared conversations.
Do not request many unrelated image edits in one prompt when precise control is needed.

faq.question: Does this skill generate images directly inside Claude, Codex, or Claude Code?
faq.answer: No. It guides the assistant to call an external image generation API and return the resulting image URL.

faq.question: Does it support image-to-image generation?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow uses an image URL at the start of the prompt, followed by the edit or style instruction.

faq.question: Where is the API key configured?
faq.answer: The documentation tells users to create a secrets.md file from the provided example and place the API key there.

faq.question: Can I use local images directly?
faq.answer: Local images must be uploaded first so the image API can access them through a URL.

faq.question: What output sizes are documented?
faq.answer: The README lists common ratios including 1:1, 16:9, 9:16, 4:3, 3:4, 3:2, 2:3, 4:5, 5:4, and 21:9.

faq.question: What should I review before publishing this skill?
faq.answer: Review the third-party API dependency, affiliate registration link, API-key handling, and public image upload guidance.
